
```{r}
set.seed(0)
pacman::p_load(cmdstanr, tidyverse, cowplot, posterior, ggh4x)
theme_set(theme_cowplot())
```

```{r}
agent.PBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + (1 - own_weighting) * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + (1 - own_weighting) * (8 - external_rating)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})

agent.WBA <- Vectorize(function(
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
    alpha_post <- alpha_prior + own_weighting * (own_rating - 1) + external_weighting * (external_rating - 1)
    beta_post  <- beta_prior  + own_weighting * (8 - own_rating) + external_weighting * (8 - external_rating)
    
    1 + sum(rbinom(1, size = 7, prob = rbeta(1, alpha_post, beta_post)))
})

simulate <- function(
    agent_evaluator,
    n_observations,
    alpha_prior,
    beta_prior,
    own_rating,
    external_rating,
    own_weighting,
    external_weighting
) {
  tibble(
    FaceID=seq(1, n_observations),
    FirstRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
    GroupRating=round(runif(n_observations, 1, 8)),
  ) |> mutate(
    SecondRating=agent_evaluator(
        alpha_prior=alpha_prior,
        beta_prior=beta_prior,
        own_rating=FirstRating,
        external_rating=GroupRating,
        own_weighting=own_weighting,
        external_weighting=external_weighting
    ),
  )
}

simulation <- simulate(
  agent_evaluator=agent.PBA,
  n_observations=50,
  alpha_prior=0.5,
  beta_prior=0.5,
  own_weighting=0.5,
  external_weighting=NA
)

simulation

add_diagnostic_columns <- function(data) {
  data |> mutate(
    Feedback=GroupRating-FirstRating,
    Change=SecondRating-FirstRating,
  )
}

plot_feedback_change_relation <- function(data) 
{
  ggplot(data) +
    geom_jitter(aes(x=Feedback, y=Change), width=0.1, height=0.1, alpha=0.5, shape=1) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7), limits=c(-7.5, 7.5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7), limits=c(-7.5, 7.5)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept=0, alpha=0.2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=0, alpha=0.2) +
    coord_fixed()
}

plot_feedback_change_relation(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))
```



```{r fig.width = 10}
plot_participant_behaviour <- function(data) {
  ggplot(data) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating, yend=SecondRating), 
    arrow=arrow(type="closed", length = unit(0.2, "cm"))
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=FirstRating), 
    color="black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(x=FaceID, y=GroupRating),
    color="red"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  labs(y="Ratings (arrow to second rating, red denotes group rating)")
}

plot_participant_behaviour(add_diagnostic_columns(simulation))
```





```{r}
add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(FirstRating, GroupRating, SecondRating),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Rating",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Rating)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1, 8)) +
  facet_wrap(~Observation) +
  labs(y="Observation count")

add_diagnostic_columns(simulation) |> pivot_longer(
  cols=c(Feedback, Change),
  names_to="Observation",
  values_to="Offset",
) |> ggplot(aes(x=Offset)) +
  geom_bar() +
  stat_summary(aes(xintercept = after_stat(x), y = 0), fun = mean, geom = "vline", orientation = "y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(-7, 7), limits=c(-7, 7)) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(Observation, c("Feedback", "Change"))) +
  labs(y="Observation count")
```

```{r}

## Setting up agent types

# agents_wba <- tibble(
#   AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
#   own_weighting = c(1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
#   external_weighting = c(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)
# )
# 
# agents_pba <- tibble(
#   AgentType    = c("Self-Focused", "Balanced", "Socially-Influenced"),
#   own_weighting = c(1.5, 1.0, 0.5),
#   external_weighting = NA
# )
# 
# ## Global params 
# n_observations <- 100
# alpha_prior    <- 4
# beta_prior     <- 3
# 
# ## adding columns to identify agent type and ModelType in the results
# run_sim <- function(agent_evaluator, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType) {
#   simulate(
#     agent_evaluator    = agent_evaluator,
#     n_observations     = n_observations,
#     alpha_prior        = alpha_prior,
#     beta_prior         = beta_prior,
#     own_weighting      = own_weighting,
#     external_weighting = external_weighting
#   ) |> mutate(
#     Feedback   = GroupRating - FirstRating,
#     Change     = SecondRating - FirstRating,
#     AbsChange  = abs(SecondRating - FirstRating),
#     AgentType = AgentType,
#     ModelType = ModelType
#   )
# }
# 
# ## run for pba typologies
# results_pba <- agents_pba |>
#   pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
#     run_sim(agent.PBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "PBA")
#   }) |>
#   list_rbind() |> drop_na(SecondRating)
# 
# ## run for wba typologies
# results_wba <- agents_wba |>
#   pmap(function(AgentType, own_weighting, external_weighting) {
#     run_sim(agent.WBA, own_weighting, external_weighting, AgentType, ModelType = "WBA")
#   }) |>
#   list_rbind()|> drop_na(SecondRating)
# 
# ## combine
# results_all <- bind_rows(results_pba, results_wba)

```


```{r}
## Plot the combined results for each ModelType and AgentType

# for (m in unique(results_all$ModelType)) {
#   p <- results_all |>
#     filter(ModelType == m) |>
#     ggplot(aes(x = SecondRating, fill = AgentType)) +
#     geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "dodge", colour = "white") +
#     facet_wrap(~ AgentType, labeller = label_both) +
#     scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
#     labs(title    = paste("Distribution of Second Ratings —", m),
#       x = "Second Rating",
#       y = "Count",
#       fill = "Agent Type") +
#     theme(legend.position = "bottom")
#   
#   print(p)
# }

```


```{r}
get_draws <- function(fit, params) { 
  as_draws_df(fit$draws()) |> 
    as_tibble() |> 
    select(all_of(params)) |> 
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "value") |> 
    mutate(
      source = if_else(str_detect(parameter, "_prior$"), "Prior", "Posterior"),
      parameter = str_remove(parameter, "_prior$"),
    )
}

cogsci_data_observations <- read_csv("./data/cogsci_clean.csv") |> filter(FirstRating != 0, GroupRating != 0, SecondRating != 0) |> filter(ID<=10)

fits <- list()
draws <- tibble()
diagnostics <- tibble()
loos <- list()

models <- list(
  PBA=cmdstan_model("./stan/PBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE)),
  WBA=cmdstan_model("./stan/WBA.stan", cpp_options=list(stan_threads=FALSE))
)

for (participant_id in unique(cogsci_data_observations$ID)) {
  cogsci_data_observations.participant <- cogsci_data_observations |> filter(ID==participant_id)
  particpant_data = list(
    N=nrow(cogsci_data_observations.participant),
    FirstRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$FirstRating,
    GroupRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$GroupRating,
    SecondRating=cogsci_data_observations.participant$SecondRating
  )

  for (model in names(models)) {
    fit <- models[[model]]$sample(
      data=particpant_data,
      seed = 0,
      chains = 4,
      parallel_chains = 4,
      iter_sampling = 1000,
      iter_warmup = 1000,
      refresh = 0
    )
    fits <- append(fits, fit)
    draws <- bind_rows(draws, mutate(get_draws(fit, params = everything()), model=model, ID=participant_id))
    diagnostics <- bind_rows(diagnostics, mutate(fit$sampler_diagnostics() |> as_draws_df() |> as_tibble() |> select(divergent__, .chain, .iteration, .draw), model=model, ID=participant_id))
  }
}


#OCCASIONAl WARNING:
#Chain 4 Informational Message: The current Metropolis proposal is about to be rejected because of the following issue:
#Chain 4 Exception: beta_binomial_lpmf: First prior sample size parameter is nan, but must be positive finite! (in '/var/folders/7q/nqnlmy5d1_15vrk6dv1x103h0000gn/T/RtmpVzUrzU/model-f0e52907dd3b.stan', line 33, column 4 to column 81)
#Chain 4 If this warning occurs sporadically, such as for highly constrained variable types like covariance matrices, then the sampler is fine,
#Chain 4 but if this warning occurs often then your model may be either severely ill-conditioned or misspecified.
#Chain 4 
```

```{r fig.width=14}
plot_prior_posterior <- function(draws) {
  ggplot(draws, aes(x = value, fill = source, colour = source)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.7) +
    facet_grid2(rows=vars(ID), cols=vars(parameter), scales = "free", independent = "y") +
    labs(
      title = "Prior–posterior updates per participant",
      x = NULL, y = "Density", fill = NULL, colour = NULL
    )
}

plot_prior_posterior(draws |> filter(model == "PBA", parameter %in% c("p")))
plot_prior_posterior(draws |> filter(model == "WBA", parameter %in% c("rho", "kappa")))
```
